title: Virtualization, Containers, VDI, and Isolation Boundaries · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for virtualization, containers, vdi, and isolation boundaries and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for virtual workload placement and evidence plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, VDI, and remote sessions.
Match CPU extensions, cores, memory, storage, graphics, network, firmware, operating system, licensing, and workload concurrency to host capacity.
Trace virtual adapters, switches, modes, addresses, routes, shared folders, virtual disks, thin provisioning, snapshots, and underlying failure domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Virtualization Model · changed case · Quiz Virtualization Model
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A lab treats VMs, containers, snapshots, VDI, device passthrough, and disposable sandboxes as equivalent isolation and recovery mechanisms. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, VDI, and remote sessions.

Which action demonstrates Virtualization Model without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Virtualization Model · changed case · Quiz Virtualization Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, vdi, and remote sessions. For CompTIA A+ support Virtualization Model · changed case · Quiz Virtualization Model, keep Virtualization Model connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A lab treats VMs, containers, snapshots, VDI, device passthrough, and disposable sandboxes as equivalent isolation and recovery mechanisms. The quiz variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Distinguish type 1 and type 2 hypervisors, guests, virtual hardware, images, snapshots, clones, containers, sandboxes, VDI, and remote sessions.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: NIST Security for Full Virtualization Technologies — Controls hypervisor, guest, image, virtual-network, isolation, and operational security boundaries.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Virtual Fault Evidence, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Virtual Fault Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Virtual Fault Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A lab treats VMs, containers, snapshots, VDI, device passthrough, and disposable sandboxes as equivalent isolation and recovery mechanisms. The quiz variation 3 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Separate host, hypervisor, guest, image, resource, virtual network, storage, time, integration-tool, and application causes with reversible tests.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Virtual Fault Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Restarting the guest proves the defect was inside the guest operating system.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using separate and host plus specific case evidence.
